files.content: This file — see root object for full Strategyzer Value Proposition Canvas.

framework_used: Strategyzer Value Proposition Canvas v2 (Osterwalder & Pigneur)
sections: Customer Profile — Gains (Required / Expected / Desired / Unexpected)
Fit Analysis — Strong Fits with Competitive Moat Ratings
Gap Analysis — Unaddressed Pains & Under-served Gains

metadata.customer_segment: Engineering leads and product teams in regulated industries (medical devices, automotive, avionics, railways, IoT) building software-intensive products who need autonomous AI agents that maintain compliance and amplify human judgment.
sub_segments: Medical device software teams (IEC 62304 / ISO 13485 / FDA)
Embedded firmware & PCB hardware teams (ISO 26262, IEC 61508)
ML / data science practitioners building production pipelines
Full-stack product engineering teams (SWE + devops + QA)
Solo founders and small teams building 0→1 software products
Technical product managers and product strategists
metadata.next_review_trigger: After 5 user interviews validating HITL compliance fit, or after GAP-01/GAP-02 shipped

functional_jobs.job: Triage and root-cause errors, logs, and production signals quickly and accurately
functional_jobs.context: On-call engineers spend hours manually correlating signals; they need root-cause in minutes, not hours

functional_jobs.job: Generate, review, and approve code changes with documented, auditable review trails
functional_jobs.context: Regulated teams cannot merge without traceable, structured review evidence for auditors

functional_jobs.job: Run compliance-grade multi-step workflows: plan → human approval → implement → verify
functional_jobs.context: ISO 13485, IEC 62304, ISO 26262, DO-178C all require human-in-the-loop sign-off; no shortcuts

functional_jobs.job: Produce compliance-ready documentation: DHFs, SRS, test reports, risk registers
functional_jobs.context: Regulated teams must deliver auditable artifacts for FDA submissions and notified-body inspections

functional_jobs.job: Onboard an AI capability to a new domain (firmware, ML, PCB, docs) without building from scratch
functional_jobs.context: Domain-specific AI setup takes weeks; teams need YAML-first rapid configuration, not custom Python

functional_jobs.job: Maintain institutional knowledge as team members rotate in and out
functional_jobs.context: Domain expertise is siloed in individuals; agents should compound that knowledge, not lose it

functional_jobs.job: Orchestrate cross-domain product builds (firmware + ML + web + QA) as one coordinated workflow
functional_jobs.context: Multi-domain products require multiple specialist agents working in parallel waves, not sequentially

functional_jobs.job: Track AI agent skill improvement over time with statistically meaningful metrics
functional_jobs.context: Teams need evidence that their AI agents are measurably improving to justify continued investment

functional_jobs.job: Switch or test multiple LLM providers without rearchitecting the stack
functional_jobs.context: Model landscape changes monthly; vendor lock-in is an operational and cost risk

functional_jobs.job: Generate strategy artifacts — PRDs, roadmaps, GTM plans — that connect directly to execution
functional_jobs.context: PMs need structured strategy outputs that translate into agent task graphs, not blank-page LLM chats

social_emotional_jobs.job: Feel confident that AI suggestions won't cause regulatory or safety violations
Demonstrate to auditors and regulators that AI decisions are traceable and human-approved
Show leadership that AI investment is accelerating delivery, not creating technical or compliance risk
Avoid context-switching between 10+ different AI tools — want one stable platform to invest in
Feel that the system gets smarter over time rather than repeating the same mistakes
Maintain human authority — agents assist and propose; humans decide. Always.

extreme.pain: AI systems that act without human approval create compliance liability and erode trust — teams revert to manual workflows after incidents
extreme.consequence: Regulatory non-compliance, recall risk, legal liability, and permanent AI adoption stall in safety-critical products
extreme.frequency: Every time a generic AI tool is deployed in a regulated context

extreme.pain: Generic LLM assistants (ChatGPT, Copilot) have no domain toolchain awareness — they describe firmware changes but cannot compile or verify them
extreme.consequence: Hallucinated outputs pass code review but fail at build time; engineers still do all domain verification manually

extreme.pain: Compliance and audit documentation consumes 20–40% of engineering time in regulated domains
extreme.consequence: Delayed releases, missed regulatory deadlines, costly notified-body resubmissions

significant.pain: Institutional knowledge evaporates when key engineers leave — no compounding memory across decisions
significant.consequence: New team members repeat solved problems; domain onboarding takes months instead of days

significant.pain: AI tool fragmentation: firmware uses IAR/GCC agents, ML uses Jupyter-integrated tools, PCB uses KiCad plugins — no unified agent layer
significant.consequence: Domain-specific AI adoption is piecemeal, inconsistent, and creates 5–10 separate maintenance burdens

significant.pain: No feedback loop from human corrections back to AI — the same analysis mistakes repeat across sessions
significant.consequence: Teams lose confidence in the AI; adoption stalls; agents remain stuck at initial quality level

significant.pain: Adding a new agent role, task type, or domain requires Python code changes and full redeployment
significant.consequence: Teams that need firmware + PCB + ML runners must maintain three separate codebases or wait for engineering bandwidth

significant.pain: Building a 0→1 product requires assembling and coordinating separate specialist tools for each domain role
significant.consequence: Coordination overhead consumes more time than the actual work; small teams cannot orchestrate at scale

moderate.pain: LLM cost unpredictability when scaling AI to entire engineering teams
moderate.consequence: Budgeting is impossible; usage spikes create surprise bills; procurement blocks adoption

moderate.pain: Multi-step workflows lose state when a service restarts mid-execution
moderate.consequence: Long-running builds (firmware compile + test + doc generation) require manual restart and re-verification

moderate.pain: No consistent way to measure whether AI agents are improving quality over time
moderate.consequence: Cannot justify AI investment to stakeholders; no basis for agent curriculum or training investment

moderate.pain: Strategy artifacts (PRDs, roadmaps) are disconnected from the execution layer — strategy and execution drift
moderate.consequence: PMs write PRDs; engineers interpret them differently; planned features are not what gets built

required.gain: Every agent action produces a tamper-evident audit trail ready for regulatory inspection
required.metric: Zero manual audit prep time; trace_id links every proposal to its approval

required.gain: Human-in-the-loop approval gate before any consequential agent action executes — not configurable away
required.metric: 100% approval gate coverage on all solution-level agent proposals

required.gain: Domain-specific AI behavior configurable via YAML, not Python code
required.metric: New domain live in < 2 hours; zero Python changes required

expected.gain: Agents execute real domain toolchain operations — compile, DRC, train, verify — not just describe them
expected.metric: Verifiable artifacts per task: ARM binary with size/stack metrics, Gerber files, model checkpoint with eval scores

expected.gain: System knowledge compounds — each human correction improves future agent proposals without retraining
expected.metric: Vector memory recall rate; measurable reduction in repeated errors across sessions

expected.gain: One unified platform for all domains — firmware, ML, PCB, strategy, UX, SWE — not 10 separate tools
expected.metric: Single deployment; shared approval UI; unified audit log across all domains

expected.gain: Analysis-to-decision cycle compressed from hours to minutes
expected.metric: 5-phase lean loop latency; proposal generated and queued for approval in < 2 minutes

desired.gain: Measurable agent skill improvement over time via statistically calibrated ratings
desired.metric: Glicko-2 rating trending upward; rating deviation shrinking (confidence increasing)

desired.gain: A solo founder or small team can operate with the throughput of a 20-person engineering org
desired.metric: 10x output per engineer; 0→1 product delivered in days, not months

desired.gain: Private proprietary skill IP (YAML) versioned, reusable across clients, never exposed publicly
desired.metric: SAGE_SKILLS_DIR isolation; zero skill IP in public SAGE repo

desired.gain: LLM provider swappable at runtime with zero code changes or downtime
desired.metric: POST /llm/switch completes in < 1s; no proposal gate required

unexpected_delight.gain: Agents self-improve through spaced repetition training without any human involvement
unexpected_delight.metric: Agents improve Glicko-2 rating from 1000 baseline through autonomous gym sessions

unexpected_delight.gain: 470 industry-grade exercise seeds expandable to 50,000+ domain training scenarios via LLM generation
unexpected_delight.metric: Exercise catalog grows on demand; domain difficulty auto-calibrates to agent success rate

products_and_services.name: FastAPI Agent Gateway (155 endpoints, 25 categories)
products_and_services.description: Single public interface: analysis, proposals, approvals, builds, gym, skills, LLM switching, evals, multi-tenant routing

products_and_services.name: HITL Approval Engine + SQLite Audit Log
products_and_services.description: Two-tier proposal store (risk-classified, expiry-aware); mandatory gate for solution-level proposals; immediate execution for framework control ops; per-solution audit_log.db

products_and_services.name: Vector Memory + Compounding Context (ChromaDB)
products_and_services.description: Per-solution knowledge store; CRUD + bulk import; Phase 5 feedback ingestion on every rejection; Memento-principle compounding

products_and_services.name: Open<Role> Runner Architecture (8 runners, BaseRunner interface)
products_and_services.description: OpenSWE (software), OpenFW (firmware), OpenEDA (PCB), OpenSim (SPICE/Verilog), OpenML (ML), OpenDoc (compliance docs), OpenDesign (UX), OpenStrategy (PRDs/roadmaps)

products_and_services.name: Build Orchestrator (0→1→N Pipeline)
products_and_services.description: 13-domain detection via DOMAIN_RULES; WORKFORCE_REGISTRY (19 agents, 5 teams); 32 task types; Q-learning adaptive router; actor-critic review; wave-parallel execution; anti-drift checkpoints

products_and_services.name: Agent Gym (MuZero-style self-play, Glicko-2, spaced repetition)
products_and_services.description: Play → grade → N-critic critique → reflect → compound loop; Glicko-2 ratings with RD + volatility; spaced repetition for failed exercises; adaptive exercise selection (3 tiers)

products_and_services.name: Scalable Exercise Catalog (~470 seeds → 50,000+ variants)
products_and_services.description: 8 domains, 10 variant axes per domain; LLM-generated variants persisted in SQLite; difficulty auto-calibration based on agent success rates

products_and_services.name: N-Provider Multi-Critic (Gemini + Claude + Ollama + any provider)
products_and_services.description: Parallel LLM critique; weighted aggregation (primary 1.5x); disagreement flagging (>20pt gap); flaw deduplication across providers

products_and_services.name: Modular Skill Marketplace (8 public YAML skill families)
products_and_services.description: JD-sourced; hot-reload; public/private/disabled visibility tiers; role-mapped; version-aware; SAGE_SKILLS_DIR for private IP isolation

products_and_services.name: 3-Tier Sandboxed Execution Cascade
products_and_services.description: OpenShell (NVIDIA container, YAML security policies, SSH-based) → SandboxRunner (local branch clone) → OpenSWE (direct LLM); graceful degradation

products_and_services.name: YAML-First Solution Model (project.yaml + prompts.yaml + tasks.yaml)
products_and_services.description: 3 files fully describe any agent team for any domain; no Python required; hot-reload; /onboarding/generate LLM wizard generates all 3 from plain language

products_and_services.name: Multi-Provider LLM Gateway (5 providers, 4 require no API key)
products_and_services.description: Gemini CLI, Claude Code CLI, Ollama, local GGUF, generic-CLI; thread-safe singleton; runtime hot-swap via POST /llm/switch

products_and_services.name: React 18 Dashboard (16+ modules, per-solution theming, guided tour)
products_and_services.description: Approval inbox, live console, stats strip (10s polling), org graph, gym leaderboard; 5-area accordion nav; role-based access control; solution-scoped branding

pain_relievers.reliever: HITL approval gate is architecturally enforced — not a feature flag, not configurable away. Solution-level proposals cannot execute without human sign-off. The code path does not exist.

Domain runners execute real toolchain operations: OpenFW cross-compiles ARM binaries with size/stack metrics; OpenEDA runs DRC/ERC and generates Gerbers; OpenML trains and evaluates models. Not simulated output — verifiable artifacts.

Audit log auto-captures every proposal, approval, rejection, and correction with trace_id. Zero manual documentation overhead for compliance evidence. Per-solution isolation (.sage/audit_log.db) means the artifact travels with the solution.

Vector memory stores every decision with reasoning. Future agents retrieve prior decisions as enriched context. Memento principle: behavioral improvement via retrieval, not retraining. Institutional knowledge survives team changes.

8 domain runners under one BaseRunner interface; unified API, shared approval UI, single audit log. One SAGE deployment covers firmware + ML + PCB + strategy + SWE — no separate tool maintenance per domain.

SAGE Lean Loop Phase 5 (Compound): every human rejection triggers feedback ingestion into vector memory. The rejection reasoning — not just 'rejected' — enriches future proposal context. Same mistake cannot repeat silently.
YAML-first solution config: add roles (prompts.yaml), task types (tasks.yaml), and skills (YAML files) with zero Python changes. Hot-reload via POST /skills/reload. New domain live in hours.

Build Orchestrator assembles the right workforce automatically from a plain-language description. 13-domain detection → WORKFORCE_REGISTRY selects agents and teams → task graph with 32 types → wave-parallel execution.

Ollama and local GGUF providers run fully offline at zero API cost. Provider hot-swap means cost-sensitive teams dial down to free tiers instantly. Langfuse observability (Phase 0) scaffolded for per-call cost tracking.
Anti-drift checkpoints (_checkpoint() after each state, _restore_runs() on startup) + Temporal durable workflows (LangGraph fallback). Long-running builds survive restarts without losing state.
Agent Gym Glicko-2 ratings with RD (uncertainty) and volatility (consistency) provide statistically calibrated benchmarks. GET /gym/analytics: trends, weaknesses, improvement rate. Measurable AI ROI.
OpenStrategy runner generates structured PRD/roadmap artifacts that feed directly into Build Orchestrator task graph decomposition. Strategy → execution is one API call, not a manual handoff.

gain_creators.creator: Per-solution SQLite audit log (.sage/audit_log.db) + mandatory two-tier HITL gate. Regulatory-grade compliance record auto-generated with zero developer overhead. Travels with the solution, not the framework.
3-file YAML configuration (starter template → copy → customize → run) + /onboarding/generate LLM wizard that generates all 3 YAMLs from a plain-language description. New domain live in < 2 hours.

Domain runners execute and verify real artifacts: ARM binary with size/stack/heap metrics (OpenFW), Gerber + BOM + DRC report (OpenEDA), trained model + eval metrics (OpenML), DHF + compliance cross-reference (OpenDoc).

MuZero-style reflect loop: play → grade → N-critic critique → reflect → compound into vector memory. Each training session's learnings enrich the next proposal's context. Flat inference cost, compounding quality.
8 Open<Role> runners under BaseRunner + shared FastAPI gateway + unified approval UI + single audit log. One SAGE instance handles the entire engineering org regardless of domain mix.

5-phase SAGE Lean Loop (Surface → Contextualize → Propose → Decide → Compound). Vector memory at Phase 2 eliminates cold-start. Parallel wave execution at Phase 3-4. Proposal queued in < 2 minutes from signal.

Glicko-2 ratings with RD (uncertainty shrinks with data), volatility (consistency tracking), confidence intervals (rating ± 2×RD), and streak detection. /gym/analytics: improvement rate, weakness breakdown, curriculum progression.
Build Orchestrator assembles 19 agents in 5 teams, executing 32 task types in parallel waves with Q-learning routing that learns agent strengths. Solo founder gets full-team throughput on demand.

SAGE_SKILLS_DIR + SAGE_SOLUTIONS_DIR env vars mount private IP at startup without ever touching the public SAGE repo. visibility: private skills never exported. Full IP isolation for proprietary domain expertise.
POST /llm/switch is a framework control operation — executes immediately, no proposal gate. Thread-safe singleton gateway handles the swap in < 1s. Ollama/local GGUF = zero cost fallback.

Agent Gym spaced repetition (failed exercises: +1, +3, +7, +15, +30 session intervals) + adaptive selection (optimal zone 40-70% success rate) + N-critic grading. Agents improve autonomously without human-driven training sessions.

POST /gym/catalog/generate triggers LLM variant generation from 470 industry-grade seeds × 10 variant axes × 8 domains. Variants persisted in SQLite. Domain difficulty auto-calibrates to agent success rates.

fit_gap_analysis.fit_summary: SAGE achieves strong-to-critical fit on its three highest-stakes pain points: HITL compliance enforcement, real domain toolchain execution, and compounding institutional memory. These are structurally unreachable by generic LLM assistants. Fit weakens in cost visibility, non-technical self-service onboarding, and making the compounding intelligence story visible in the UI.

strong_fits.label: Critical Pain → Structural Pain Reliever
strong_fits.description: HITL approval gate is architecturally enforced — not a config option, not a feature flag. The code path for unapproved solution-level execution does not exist. This directly addresses the #1 adoption blocker in regulated industries: AI acting without accountability.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: CRITICAL — no generic LLM assistant (Copilot, ChatGPT, Cursor) enforces this at platform level. Requires architectural commitment that cannot be bolted on.
strong_fits.evidence: Two-tier proposal store; SQLite audit_log.db per solution; trace_id on every event; SOUL.md Law 1 enshrined in code

strong_fits.label: Critical Pain → Domain-Specific Pain Reliever
strong_fits.description: Domain runners execute real toolchain operations. This closes the 'hallucination gap' — agents don't describe what to do, they do it and return verifiable domain artifacts. OpenFW cross-compiles; OpenEDA runs DRC/ERC; OpenML trains and evaluates.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: HIGH — requires deep domain engineering per runner (compiler toolchains, EDA rules, ML pipelines). Each runner adds meaningful switching cost. Hard to replicate at breadth.
strong_fits.evidence: 8 BaseRunner subclasses; execute() + verify() + grade_exercise() per runner; Docker toolchain images per domain

strong_fits.label: Extreme Pain → Automatic Pain Reliever
strong_fits.description: Per-solution audit log auto-captures all compliance evidence. IEC 62304 / ISO 13485 teams spend 20-40% of release cycle on audit prep — SAGE eliminates the manual documentation portion entirely.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: MEDIUM — audit logging itself is replicable; SAGE's moat is the per-solution isolation model (.sage/ travels with solution, not framework) and the dual-purpose design (compliance record + AI training signal).
strong_fits.evidence: .sage/audit_log.db per solution; SAGE_SOLUTIONS_DIR isolation; every event has trace_id, tenant_id, timestamp

strong_fits.label: High Pain → Compounding Pain Reliever
strong_fits.description: Vector memory + Phase 5 feedback compounding is the Memento principle in production. Every rejection writes the human's reasoning — not just 'rejected' — back into the retrieval context. Institutional knowledge accumulates and compounds with each interaction.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: HIGH — compounding memory across sessions is architecturally unique. Competitors cache outputs; SAGE compounds decisions.
strong_fits.evidence: ChromaDB per solution; bulk import; Phase 5 in SAGE Lean Loop; rejection feedback format in audit_log

strong_fits.label: High Pain → Consolidation Pain Reliever
strong_fits.description: Single platform for 8+ domain runners eliminates tool fragmentation. One deployment, one approval UI, one audit log for a team doing firmware + ML + PCB + strategy work simultaneously.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: MEDIUM — breadth of domain coverage is the moat. Each additional runner increases the platform's stickiness and the cost of switching away.
strong_fits.evidence: BaseRunner ABC with 4 required methods; runner registry (get_runner_for_role); 8 runners all under same API surface

strong_fits.label: Desired Gain → Measurable Gain Creator
strong_fits.description: Glicko-2 ratings with statistical confidence intervals are the first sports-science-grade skill measurement applied to AI agents. This is not a 'score' — it's a calibrated rating with uncertainty quantification that shrinks as data accumulates.
strong_fits.competitive_moat: HIGH — no competing agent framework has a principled skill measurement system. This enables a unique 'agent performance management' narrative for enterprise buyers.
strong_fits.evidence: SkillRating dataclass; Glicko-2 update algorithm; GymDB SQLite persistence; /gym/analytics endpoint

gaps.gap: No built-in LLM cost dashboard or per-solution budget controls — enterprise buyers cannot forecast or cap AI spend
gaps.impact: Blocks procurement approval; CFOs demand cost predictability before committing to platform contracts
gaps.recommendation: Add /costs endpoint with per-solution token/call tracking; surface in React Costs page; add budget_threshold per project.yaml with alert on breach. Langfuse integration is already scaffolded.

gaps.gap: Onboarding wizard exists at API level (POST /onboarding/generate) but no guided UI flow for non-technical domain owners — self-service adoption blocked for PMs and business analysts who cannot use curl
gaps.impact: The buyer (PM, domain owner, compliance officer) cannot self-serve; engineers must mediate onboarding, creating a bottleneck
gaps.recommendation: Build OnboardingWizard UI modal into a 5-step guided flow: describe domain → select compliance standards → preview generated YAML → confirm → activate solution. TourContext already has wizardOpen/closeWizard hooks.

gaps.gap: Agent Gym ratings and analytics are API-only — the compounding intelligence story is invisible to users who don't call the API directly
gaps.impact: The strongest retention driver (agents visibly improving over time) is hidden. Users churn before seeing value compound.
gaps.recommendation: Build /gym UI page: agent leaderboard with Glicko-2 ratings, rating trend sparklines, upcoming spaced repetition queue, recent session history, curriculum progression by role.

gaps.gap: No executive-facing ROI dashboard — leadership cannot see AI utilization, approval rates, quality trends, or time-saved estimates at a glance
gaps.impact: AI investment is hard to justify to CFO/CTO without measurable metrics; renewal conversations stall
gaps.recommendation: Add /reports endpoint: monthly proposals, approval/rejection rate, agent gym improvement trends, estimated hours saved, domain breakdown. Surface as executive summary page in UI.

gaps.gap: OpenStrategy runner generates PRDs and roadmaps but they are not automatically decomposed into Build Orchestrator task graphs — strategy-to-execution handoff is still manual
gaps.impact: Breaks the lean loop from strategy through execution; PM artifacts don't translate to agent action without engineer mediation
gaps.recommendation: Add POST /strategy/execute: takes OpenStrategy PRD output → auto-decomposes into Build Orchestrator task graph → submits as pending plan for HITL approval.

gaps.gap: The 1-person company north-star vision is not prominently surfaced in onboarding or documentation — solo founders and indie builders don't self-identify as the target user
gaps.impact: The most viral segment (founders, indie hackers) discovers SAGE only through word-of-mouth; not through intentional positioning
gaps.recommendation: Add 'Solo Founder' persona to onboarding wizard; create starter solution template pre-configured for 1-person SaaS company; add case study to docs highlighting 0→1 speed.

gaps.gap: Build Orchestrator Q-learning router scores are in-memory only — lost on every restart, relearning from scratch each build
gaps.impact: Adaptive routing has to relearn agent strengths from scratch after any deployment; wastes early builds on suboptimal routing
gaps.recommendation: Persist AdaptiveRouter scores dict to SQLite (.sage/router_scores.db); restore on startup via _restore_runs() pattern already used for build state.

action_items.title: LLM Cost Dashboard — per-solution token tracking + budget threshold alerts
action_items.rationale: Closes the enterprise procurement blocker. Langfuse integration already scaffolded in llm_gateway.py — lower effort than it appears.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: GET /costs returns per-solution daily token/call breakdown; budget_threshold in project.yaml triggers alert event in audit log; React Costs page renders usage chart

action_items.title: OnboardingWizard UI — 5-step guided flow for non-technical domain owners
action_items.rationale: TourContext already has wizardOpen/closeWizard hooks; wizard component is referenced but not fully implemented. High confidence on effort estimate.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: PM can describe a domain in plain language, select compliance standards, preview generated YAML, and activate a new solution — entirely in UI with no curl or CLI required

action_items.title: Agent Gym UI Page — leaderboard, rating trend sparklines, spaced repetition queue
action_items.rationale: Makes the strongest retention driver (compounding agent intelligence) visible. All data is available via existing /gym/* endpoints.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: User can see all agent Glicko-2 ratings, 30-day trend chart, upcoming spaced repetition exercises, and recent session history in the UI without calling any API directly

action_items.title: Executive ROI Dashboard — approval rates, agent improvement trends, time-saved estimates
action_items.rationale: Critical for renewal conversations. Audit log already contains all raw data; aggregation layer needed.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: CTO-level summary page: monthly proposal volume, approval rate, top agent by improvement rate, domain activity breakdown, estimated hours saved formula

action_items.title: Strategy → Execution Bridge: POST /strategy/execute → Build Orchestrator task graph
action_items.rationale: Completes the lean loop from strategy through execution. OpenStrategy runner already produces structured PRD artifacts.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: POST /strategy/execute takes a PRD artifact ID, auto-decomposes into Build Orchestrator task graph, submits as pending plan requiring HITL approval before any execution

action_items.title: Persist AdaptiveRouter Q-learning scores to SQLite for cross-restart retention
action_items.rationale: Low-effort, high-correctness fix. Router already has the scores dict; just needs SQLite persistence using the existing _restore_runs pattern.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: Router scores survive service restart; second build routes to same agents as first without re-learning period; scores visible in GET /build/router/stats

action_items.title: Solo Founder Starter Template + persona in OnboardingWizard
action_items.rationale: Lowest effort item; highest viral potential. Founders share tools that make them feel like a 20-person team.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: Solo Founder persona option in onboarding wizard; starter template includes single-founder-optimized active_modules list and prompts; case study added to docs

action_items.title: User Interview Program — 5 regulated-industry engineering leads to validate FIT-01 as primary sales wedge
action_items.rationale: HITL compliance fit is the sharpest differentiator. Validate that it is the primary purchase driver before investing in go-to-market messaging.
action_items.acceptance_criteria: 5 interviews completed; HITL compliance ranked as top-3 purchase driver in 4+ interviews; secondary drivers documented for messaging hierarchy

vpc_summary_card.headline_value_proposition: SAGE is the only agentic AI framework that executes real domain toolchains (firmware, PCB, ML, strategy) with architecturally enforced human approval and a self-improving memory layer — purpose-built for teams where AI errors carry regulatory or safety consequences.
top_3_fits: FIT-01 — Architecturally enforced HITL gate (P-01 → PR-01): zero generic AI tool offers this; it is the compliance moat that cannot be bolted on
FIT-02 — Real toolchain execution via domain runners (P-02 → PR-02): agents produce verifiable artifacts, not hallucinated descriptions of what to do
FIT-04 — Compounding vector memory from human feedback (P-04/P-06 → PR-04/PR-06): institutional knowledge survives team rotation and compounds over time
top_3_gaps_to_close: GAP-01 — Cost visibility dashboard: blocks enterprise procurement without per-solution budget tracking
GAP-02 — Non-technical onboarding UI: self-service adoption requires a guided wizard, not just a POST endpoint
GAP-03 — Agent Gym UI page: the compounding intelligence story must be visible, not hidden behind API calls
vpc_summary_card.positioning_statement: For engineering teams in regulated or complex software domains, SAGE is the agentic AI platform that combines domain-specialist execution, mandatory human oversight, and compounding institutional memory — unlike generic LLM assistants that hallucinate domain actions and create compliance liability.
